scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. ChartMogul publishes REST resources for account, sources, customers, contacts, notes/call-logs, opportunities, tasks, plans, plan groups, subscriptions/subscription events, invoices, line items, transactions, bulk import, metrics, and activities. This bundle covers every practical GET list/detail resource that is a durable business object reachable with a plain API key AND the customer create/update write pair (the only mutation this dialect can safely and completely express against ChartMogul's real request shape without a CRM-seat-gated credential). Genuinely out-of-scope surface is documented per-endpoint below with a specific reason, never a blanket bucket. ChartMogul remains read-only for legacy parity streams; customer writes are new Pass B capability.

excluded.reason: data-source (integration connection) inventory is account-configuration metadata describing where customer data originates, not itself customer/business data; no legacy precedent
creating a new data source is a one-time account-setup action, not a routine sync-driven mutation
same as the sources list — account-configuration metadata
deleting a data source cascades to delete every customer/subscription/invoice imported from it; an irreversible account-structure action
accepts a raw CSV file upload, not a structured JSON business-data write
single-customer read is redundant with the customers list stream, which already emits every field the detail endpoint returns
a one-time data-deduplication action operating on two existing customer records, not a routine create/update of business data
reverses a prior merge; a rare data-remediation action, not routine sync-driven business data

adding a contact to a specific customer is equivalent to the general create_contact write surface (POST /v1/contacts with a customer_uuid), which this Pass B pass does not cover for contacts (see contacts entries below); scoping create/update to customers only for this pass
irreversible deletion of a customer and its full activity/subscription history
per-customer activity filtering is already reachable by client-side filtering the activities stream's customer-uuid field; no data unavailable elsewhere
email-based customer search returns a subset of the same records the customers list stream already emits in full
per-customer invoice filtering is already reachable by client-side filtering the invoices stream's customer_uuid field
tag/attribute management is a narrow metadata-tagging action distinct from the customer record itself; Pass B's representative write scope covers the create/update customer pair only
same as per-customer tag add — narrow metadata-tagging action, out of Pass B's representative write scope
tag removal is the same narrow metadata action as tag add
custom-attribute management is a narrow metadata action distinct from the customer record itself
same as per-customer attribute add
tags/attributes retrieval is a sub-view of data already reachable via the customer detail; no independent business object
custom-attribute management is a narrow metadata action distinct from the customer record itself
same narrow metadata action as attribute add/update
contact create/update is a full parallel CRM-object write surface beyond Pass B's representative scope, which prioritizes the customer object itself; contacts remain read-only via the contacts stream
single-contact read is redundant with the contacts list stream
same as contact create — out of this pass's representative write scope
one-time data-deduplication action, not routine business-data create/update
irreversible deletion of a contact record
CRM notes/call-logs are a narrow CRM-module object; Pass B prioritizes the core billing/customer surface over the full CRM object graph
same CRM-module scoping as the notes create above
CRM notes/call-logs are a narrow CRM-module object read; deferred alongside the notes write surface
same CRM-module scoping as notes create/read
irreversible deletion of a CRM note/call-log record
ChartMogul's own docs require an API key created by a user with a CRM seat to use the Opportunities endpoints; not every account/API key can reach this surface
same CRM-seat-gated credential requirement as the opportunities write/list endpoints
requires a CRM-seat-gated API key per ChartMogul's own documentation; a plain billing-data API key cannot reach this endpoint
same CRM-seat-gated credential requirement
same CRM-seat-gated credential requirement
task create/update is a narrow CRM-module write surface; Pass B exposes tasks read-only via the tasks stream
single-task read is redundant with the tasks list stream
same narrow CRM-module write scoping as task create
irreversible deletion of a CRM task record
plan create/update is a narrow contract-pricing-catalog write surface with no legacy precedent; Pass B exposes plans read-only via the plans stream
single-plan read is redundant with the plans list stream
same narrow catalog-write scoping as plan create
irreversible deletion of a plan definition every associated subscription references
plan-group (bundle) definition is a narrower catalog-organization feature with no legacy precedent; deferred past this pass in favor of the core plans object
same catalog-organization scoping as plan_groups create
plan-group listing is a narrower catalog-organization view over the same plans this bundle already covers individually via the plans stream
a plan-group's member plans are the same plan records already emitted in full by the plans stream
same catalog-organization scoping as plan_groups create
irreversible deletion of a plan-group definition

subscriptions have no top-level site-wide list endpoint in ChartMogul's API (only per-customer), requiring a fan-out over every customer id; deferred pending a dotted-path-capable id_field or per-customer sub-resource fan-out design, matching the same class of gap as chargify's per-product-family components
connecting/merging imported subscription records across data sources is a one-time data-migration action, not routine business-data create/update
reverses a prior subscription connect; a rare data-remediation action
custom-attribute management on a subscription is a narrow metadata action
same narrow metadata scoping as the attribute write above
same narrow metadata scoping
same narrow metadata scoping

subscription-event ingestion is ChartMogul's event-sourcing write API for building up subscription state incrementally — a fundamentally different write model (event stream, not object CRUD) than every other action in this bundle; out of Pass B's representative CRUD-shaped write scope
raw subscription-event log is billing-system-internal event-sourcing data, not a durable business object comparable to the other streams this bundle exposes
same event-sourcing scoping as the subscription_events entries above
same event-sourcing scoping
deleting a subscription event can retroactively change historical MRR/ARR calculations derived from it

invoice import is a bulk historical-data-loading action (accepts an array of invoices with nested line items), a fundamentally different write shape than this dialect's per-record create actions; not part of Pass B's representative write scope
single-invoice read is redundant with the invoices list stream
manual invoice status override bypasses ChartMogul's normal invoice-import lifecycle; a support/edge-case action, not routine
full invoice replace is part of the same bulk-import write model as invoice creation, not a per-field object update
disabling an invoice from MRR calculations is a narrow accounting-correction action
irreversible deletion of an imported invoice record
irreversible bulk deletion of every invoice for a customer
line items only exist nested inside an invoice-import payload in practice; a standalone line-item create is not a realistic independent write this dialect's per-record body shape fits
line items are sub-objects of invoices, already emitted (where present) as nested data on the invoices stream; no independent list endpoint exists
same sub-object scoping as line_items create
narrow accounting-correction action on an invoice sub-object
irreversible deletion of an invoice line item
transactions only exist nested inside an invoice-import payload (payment/refund/failure events tied to one invoice); no standalone transaction object this dialect's per-record write shape fits
transactions are invoice sub-objects with no independent list endpoint
same sub-object scoping as transactions create
narrow accounting-correction action on an invoice sub-object
irreversible deletion of a payment transaction record
bulk-import job submission is a one-time data-migration action with an asynchronous job-tracking model fundamentally different from this dialect's synchronous per-record writes
returns transient bulk-import job status, not a durable business-data record

the all-key-metrics endpoint returns a wide multi-series object shaped for dashboard rendering, not a row-shaped collection this connector's per-stream schema model fits; customer_count already demonstrates the metrics-endpoint pattern this bundle supports

MRR is a single time-series metric endpoint; Pass B's representative metrics coverage is customer_count (already covered) rather than every individual metric endpoint, which all share the identical interval/start-date/end-date query shape and no legacy precedent for the others
same representative-metrics scoping as mrr
same representative-metrics scoping as mrr
same representative-metrics scoping as mrr
same representative-metrics scoping as mrr
same representative-metrics scoping as mrr
same representative-metrics scoping as mrr
creates an asynchronous bulk-export job, not itself a business-data write
returns transient export-job status/download metadata, not a durable business-data record
